job_order_request_details.benefits:
Full-time W-2 position with a comprehensive benefits package including:
- Medical: Employer-sponsored PPO health insurance through BlueCross BlueShield with 80% of employee premium and 50% of dependent premium covered by the employer, eligibility starting the first of the month following 30 days of employment.
- Dental & Vision: Separate dental and vision plans with preventive dental services covered at 100% and annual eye exam covered in full in-network.
- Retirement: 401(k) plan with up to 4% employer match after 90 days of employment; immediate vesting on employee contributions and graded vesting on employer contributions over 5 years.
- Paid Time Off (PTO): 15 days of PTO annually for the first two years (accrued per pay period), 8 paid company holidays, and 2 floating personal days per calendar year.
- Sick Leave: Separate bank of up to 40 hours of paid sick leave annually for personal or family illness, in compliance with state and local regulations.
- Additional Benefits: Short-term disability coverage paid by employer, optional long-term disability, basic life insurance policy equal to one year of base salary (employer paid), employee assistance program (EAP) with free confidential counseling sessions, professional development reimbursement up to $750 per year for approved courses or certifications, and commuter benefits (pre-tax transit and parking options).
job_order_request_details.duties_and_responsibilities:
Position: Customer Support Specialist – Software-as-a-Service (SaaS)

This full-time role is responsible for providing front-line customer service and technical support for a cloud-based small business accounting platform. The person in this role will serve as one of the primary points of contact for customers via phone, email, and in-app chat, and will be expected to handle a mix of “how do I?” questions, basic troubleshooting, and account-related inquiries.

Core duties and responsibilities include, but are not limited to:

1. Customer Interaction and Case Management
- Respond promptly and professionally to inbound customer inquiries through phone, email, and live chat, maintaining an average first-response time of under 1 business hour for email and under 60 seconds for chat during scheduled shifts.
- Listen carefully to customer concerns, ask clarifying questions, and restate issues in clear language to confirm understanding before proposing solutions.
- Document all interactions in the company’s ticketing/CRM system (currently Zendesk and HubSpot) with detailed notes, relevant screenshots, and appropriate categorization and tagging.
- Take ownership of each case from initial contact through resolution, following up with customers proactively if an issue requires additional research or escalation.
- Meet or exceed team performance metrics, including customer satisfaction (CSAT), average handle time (AHT), and case closure targets.

2. Technical Troubleshooting and Product Support
- Guide customers through basic troubleshooting steps such as clearing browser cache, updating their browser, checking internet connectivity, and verifying login credentials.
- Assist customers with navigation questions (e.g., how to create invoices, reconcile bank transactions, generate profit and loss reports, or add new users) by walking them through the steps on-screen.
- Use internal knowledge base and training materials to accurately answer questions related to product settings, integrations (e.g., bank feeds, payment processors), and common error messages.
- Identify patterns in recurring issues and flag potential bugs, outages, or confusing user interface elements to the Product and Engineering teams via established escalation channels.
- Participate in regular product update briefings and self-study to stay current on new features, interface changes, and known issues.

3. Account and Billing Assistance
- Help customers update account details such as contact information, company profile, and user permissions following established verification and security protocols.
- Provide basic billing support including plan changes, invoice copies, payment method updates, and explanations of charges, while adhering to company policies and approval thresholds.
- Educate customers on available plan options and add-ons without engaging in aggressive sales tactics; when appropriate, transfer customers to the Sales team for more in-depth plan consultations.

4. Customer Education and Proactive Support
- Teach customers how to use self-service resources such as the help center, tutorial videos, and in-app guided tours, with the goal of increasing customer independence over time.
- Contribute to the internal and external knowledge base by suggesting article updates, identifying missing topics, and, when assigned, drafting or revising step-by-step guides with screenshots and plain-language explanations.
- Participate in new customer onboarding webinars or small-group virtual training sessions as needed, walking participants through key workflows like setting up their chart of accounts, connecting bank feeds, and generating basic financial reports.

5. Collaboration and Continuous Improvement
- Collaborate closely with other Customer Support Specialists, Product Managers, and Quality Assurance staff to share feedback, document edge cases, and help refine troubleshooting flows.
- Attend weekly team meetings to review metrics, discuss complex cases, and provide input on process improvements.
- Follow all established procedures for security, privacy, and data protection, especially when handling financial information or personally identifiable information (PII).
- Provide constructive feedback on internal tools, scripts, and macros that could improve efficiency or customer satisfaction.

6. Administrative and Miscellaneous Tasks
- Accurately track time spent on different contact channels (phone, email, chat) and internal work (training, documentation) as required by management.
- Complete all required compliance, security, and product training by assigned deadlines.
- Support occasional special projects such as seasonal onboarding surges (e.g., at year-end or tax season), pilot programs for new features, or process documentation initiatives.
- Perform other related duties as assigned by the Customer Support Manager, consistent with the overall scope and level of the position.

Work Environment and Expectations:
- This is a hybrid role based out of the company’s Boston, MA office, with an expectation of 2–3 days per week on-site after an initial training period.
- Standard schedule is Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:30 PM Eastern Time, with a 1-hour unpaid lunch break. Some flexibility may be required during peak periods (e.g., quarter-end and year-end) for extended hours or occasional Saturday coverage, with advance notice and overtime pay in accordance with applicable laws.
- The role requires extensive computer and phone use, including simultaneous use of multiple browser tabs, internal tools, and communication channels.
